concerns.text: Task T014 mentions 'generate Synthetic BMG Dataset' as a fallback, but lacks a dedicated implementation task for the generator logic itself. The plan.md (Critical Data Contingency) and spec.md (FR-001) require a code-generated synthetic dataset to ensure reproducibility if the API fails. Without a specific task to implement the generator (e.g., 'Implement synthetic data generator based on literature parameters'), the pipeline cannot satisfy the 'Reproducibility' and 'Verified Accuracy' constitutional principles if real data is unavailable.

Task T015 mandates recording checksums to 'state/ingest.yaml'. However, Constitution Principle V (Versioning Discipline) and the Data Hygiene section explicitly require checksums to be recorded in the single canonical file 'state/projects/PROJ-380-...yaml'. Creating a separate 'state/ingest.yaml' fragments the versioning state, violating the 'Single Source of Truth' principle and the specific artifact path defined in the constitution.

Task T029 specifies implementing 'Leave-One-Family-Out (LOFO) cross-validation'. The spec.md (FR-008) and plan.md (Complexity Tracking) explicitly require a HYBRID approach: LOFO for large families and GroupKFold for small families to prevent empty folds. Implementing only LOFO fails to handle the edge case of rare alloy families, violating the requirement to ensure generalization across all families without causing pipeline failure.

Task T027 implements the 'Wilcoxon Signed-Rank Test'. The spec.md (FR-007) and User Story 2 Acceptance Scenario 3 explicitly require a 'corrected resampled t-test (Nadeau & Bengio)' or 'paired permutation test' to account for fold dependency. While the plan mentions Wilcoxon as a 'Statistical Rigor' constraint, the functional requirement (FR-007) and user story acceptance criteria prioritize the corrected t-test/permutation test for fold dependency. Relying solely on Wilcoxon without the specified fold-dependency correction constitutes a semantic weakening of the evaluation requirement.

Task T018 states it will 'flag/remove collinear features'. The plan.md (Complexity Tracking) and spec.md (FR-003) require that VIF > 5 triggers 'feature removal or PCA'. The task description's 'flagging' is insufficient; if the task only flags and does not implement the removal or PCA logic, the pipeline will proceed with collinear features, violating the 'Data Hygiene' and 'Model Evaluation Transparency' requirements for a clean feature matrix.
